name: pending-stub
description: Close review confirmations for E2 restructure chain (RESTRUCTURE-001a, 001b-1) and E15 HA gate (HA-001). Advance E13 autarky compliance (TEST-010) and extend the HA gate to all E13 testing charts (HA-010). Remove legacy phase field from 8 backlog stories (KAIZEN-004).
sprintGoal: Close the E2 restructure and HA-001 review debt, complete E13 autarky and HA gate coverage, and eliminate the last legacy phase-field drift in the backlog.

stories.title: Contract layer: cluster.schema.yaml, validate.py, test fixtures
stories.description: Create contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml defining the sovereign cluster contract invariants. Create contract/validate.py that validates a cluster config YAML against the schema and exits 1 with an 'AUTARKY VIOLATION' message on constraint failures. Create test fixtures: contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml (passes validation) and contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml (fails with AUTARKY VIOLATION).
acceptanceCriteria: contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml exists and is valid YAML: python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml'))" exits 0
python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml exits 0 with output containing 'CONTRACT VALID'
python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml exits 1 with output containing 'AUTARKY VIOLATION'
stories.testPlan: python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('contract/v1/cluster.schema.yaml'))" && echo 'schema OK'; python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/valid.yaml; python3 contract/validate.py contract/v1/tests/invalid-egress-not-blocked.yaml; show all output verbatim.

stories.title: cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh: kind cluster provisioner with --help, --cluster-name, and kind create cluster invocation
stories.description: Create cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh. The script must: (1) support --help flag that prints usage including '--cluster-name NAME (default: sovereign-test)'; (2) accept --cluster-name NAME option defaulting to 'sovereign-test'; (3) print 'Bootstrapping kind cluster: $cluster_name' before running; (4) invoke 'kind create cluster --name "$cluster_name"'. Script must pass shellcheck -S error with set -euo pipefail at the top. Also verify that bootstrap/providers/ is not present in the working tree (it should have been removed in RESTRUCTURE-001a).
acceptanceCriteria: cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exists: ls cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0
shellcheck -S error cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0
bash -n cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0
bash cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh --help 2>&1 | grep -q 'cluster-name' exits 0
grep -q 'kind create cluster' cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh exits 0 (script contains the kind invocation)
[ ! -d bootstrap/providers ] exits 0 (bootstrap/providers/ not in working tree)
stories.testPlan: ls cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && shellcheck -S error cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && bash -n cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && bash cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh --help 2>&1 | grep 'cluster-name' && grep 'kind create cluster' cluster/kind/bootstrap.sh && [ ! -d bootstrap/providers ] && echo 'all checks passed'; show all output verbatim

smart.notes: All SMART dimensions green. Revised up from prior 4/4: every file is named, every flag and output is specified precisely, all ACs are binary shell commands.

stories.title: HA gate script validates all platform charts have replicaCount>=2, PDB, and podAntiAffinity
stories.description: Create scripts/ha-gate.sh that iterates every chart in charts/ and checks: (1) values.yaml has replicaCount >= 2, (2) helm template output contains PodDisruptionBudget, (3) helm template output contains podAntiAffinity. Script exits non-zero and prints FAIL:<chart>:<reason> for each violation. Print PASS:<chart> for each chart that passes all checks. Script must pass shellcheck -S error and support --dry-run flag that lists charts without running helm. IMPORTANT FIX (from sprint-23 review): The grep pipeline for extracting replicaCount must use '|| true' to prevent set -euo pipefail from killing the script when processing charts that have no top-level replicaCount field (e.g. platform/charts/_globals/). Pattern: replica_count=$(grep -E '^replicaCount:' "$chart/values.yaml" || true | awk ...)
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ha-gate.sh exists and passes shellcheck -S error: shellcheck -S error scripts/ha-gate.sh exits 0
bash -n scripts/ha-gate.sh exits 0 (syntax check)
scripts/ha-gate.sh --dry-run prints the list of charts it will check with output containing each chart directory name, then exits 0
For a chart that has replicaCount: 1 in values.yaml, scripts/ha-gate.sh outputs FAIL:<chart>:replicaCount < 2 and exits non-zero
For a chart with replicaCount>=2, PDB, and podAntiAffinity in templates, scripts/ha-gate.sh outputs PASS:<chart> for that chart
stories.testPlan: shellcheck -S error scripts/ha-gate.sh && bash -n scripts/ha-gate.sh && scripts/ha-gate.sh --dry-run; show all output verbatim. Then create a minimal test chart with replicaCount: 1 and run scripts/ha-gate.sh against it to confirm FAIL output.

smart.notes: Measurable score 4: ACs cover shellcheck, syntax, --dry-run, and a synthetic test-chart pass/fail, but no AC requires running ha-gate.sh against the full platform/charts/ corpus. Achievable score 3: per SMART check policy, any shell script that iterates platform/charts/ must include an explicit AC to run against all existing charts (including _globals/ which has no replicaCount field). That AC is absent; the real corpus will expose the set -euo pipefail + grep no-match failure on _globals/ even if the || true guard is in the description.

stories.returnedReason: bash set -euo pipefail kills script on grep no-match for charts without replicaCount field; fix: add || true guard to grep pipeline (see sprint reviewNotes)

stories.title: All E13 Helm charts comply with autarky gate (G6) — no external registry references
stories.description: Audit all templates under platform/charts/selenium-grid/, platform/charts/k6-operator/, platform/charts/chaos-mesh/, platform/charts/mailhog/, and platform/charts/wiremock/ and replace any hard-coded external registry references with {{ .Values.global.imageRegistry }}. All five charts must pass the G6 grep check and helm lint clean.
acceptanceCriteria: grep -rn 'docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io\|gcr\.io\|registry\.k8s\.io' platform/charts/selenium-grid/templates/ returns no matches
grep -rn 'docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io\|gcr\.io\|registry\.k8s\.io' platform/charts/k6-operator/templates/ returns no matches
grep -rn 'docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io\|gcr\.io\|registry\.k8s\.io' platform/charts/chaos-mesh/templates/ returns no matches
grep -rn 'docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io\|gcr\.io\|registry\.k8s\.io' platform/charts/mailhog/templates/ returns no matches
grep -rn 'docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io\|gcr\.io\|registry\.k8s\.io' platform/charts/wiremock/templates/ returns no matches
stories.testPlan: bash -c 'result=$(grep -rn "docker\.io\|quay\.io\|ghcr\.io\|gcr\.io\|registry\.k8s\.io" platform/charts/selenium-grid/templates/ platform/charts/k6-operator/templates/ platform/charts/chaos-mesh/templates/ platform/charts/mailhog/templates/ platform/charts/wiremock/templates/ 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$result" ] && echo PASS || (echo FAIL && echo "$result" && exit 1)'

smart.notes: Achievable score 4: five charts each with potentially multiple template files means 5-15 edits; within budget but near the top.

stories.readinessNote: READY: dependencies TEST-004a, TEST-005a, TEST-006a, TEST-006b confirmed accepted (passes:true, reviewed:true) in sprint 35. All deps resolved.

stories.title: HA gate extended to cover E13 testing charts — Selenium, k6, Chaos Mesh, MailHog, Wiremock
stories.description: Update scripts/ha-gate.sh to include the five E13 testing infrastructure chart directories. All five charts must pass: replicaCount >= 2, PodDisruptionBudget present, podAntiAffinity defined. This closes the HA coverage gap for the full platform chart set.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ha-gate.sh references platform/charts/selenium-grid/, platform/charts/k6-operator/, platform/charts/chaos-mesh/, platform/charts/mailhog/, platform/charts/wiremock/ in its chart list
helm template platform/charts/selenium-grid/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget returns a match
helm template platform/charts/chaos-mesh/ | grep podAntiAffinity returns a match
helm template platform/charts/k6-operator/ | grep PodDisruptionBudget returns a match
Running ha-gate.sh --dry-run against all E13 charts exits 0
stories.testPlan: for chart in platform/charts/selenium-grid platform/charts/k6-operator platform/charts/chaos-mesh platform/charts/mailhog platform/charts/wiremock; do echo "=== $chart ==="; helm template $chart | grep -E 'PodDisruptionBudget|podAntiAffinity' | wc -l; done

smart.notes: Specific score 4: it is unclear whether chart HA config (replicaCount, PDB, podAntiAffinity) must be added or whether the dependencies already supply it; the story should state this explicitly. Measurable score 3: ACs only check PDB/podAntiAffinity for 3 of 5 charts (mailhog and wiremock have no explicit helm template AC); no AC verifies replicaCount >= 2 for any E13 chart; no AC runs ha-gate.sh (non-dry-run) against the real E13 charts.

stories.readinessNote: READY: HA-001 passes:true; TEST-004a, TEST-005a, TEST-006a, TEST-006b confirmed accepted (passes:true, reviewed:true) in sprint 35. All dependencies resolved.

stories.title: Remove legacy `phase` field from 8 backlog stories added after KAIZEN-006
stories.description: KAIZEN-006 removed the `phase` field from all backlog stories, but 8 stories added after that run still carry `phase`: DEVEX-012, DEVEX-013, QUALITY-008, QUALITY-009, TEST-009, TEST-010, HA-009, HA-010. Remove the `phase` field from each. The field is redundant with `epicId` (which implies targetIncrement) and its presence is a constitutional violation flagged by the coherence audit.
acceptanceCriteria: python3 -c "import json; b=json.load(open('prd/backlog.json')); bad=[s['id'] for s in b['stories'] if 'phase' in s]; assert bad==[], f'stories with phase field: {bad}'; print('PASS')" exits 0 and prints PASS
stories.testPlan: python3 -c "import json; b=json.load(open('prd/backlog.json')); bad=[s['id'] for s in b['stories'] if 'phase' in s]; assert bad==[], f'stories with phase field: {bad}'; print('PASS')"
